text: This is a seventy-something-year-old female with a little bit of adhesions from prior surgery, but acute cholecystitis admitted overnight again, stone seen on ultrasound, a little bit of pericholecystic fluid, but nothing crazy.
The surgeon performs infundibular dissection using the robotic prograsp and arm number one, focusing on dissecting out the cystic duct. The cystic artery is visible with a clear view.
The surgeon attempts to grasp small branching vessels near the cystic duct using robotic instruments, noting minor bleeding as clips are applied to secure them.

The surgeon obtains the critical view of safety, with the liver margin visible in the background. A crossing vessel exhibits minor bleeding, which is controlled using cautery. The surgeon works up the cystic plate in a straightforward manner.
